name: Bull Moose Jackson
description: American blues and rhythm-and-blues singer and saxophonist (1919-1989)
abstract: Benjamin Clarence "Bull Moose" Jackson (April 22, 1919 – July 31, 1989) was an American blues and rhythm-and-blues singer and saxophonist, who was most successful in the late 1940s. He is considered a performer of dirty blues because of the suggestive nature of some of his songs, such as "I Want a Bowlegged Woman" and "Big Ten Inch Recor…

works.t: I Love You, Yes I Do

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: The Johnny Otis Show

about_quotes.speaker: Norris Turney
about_quotes.evidence: I remember when it first started I was in Philadelphia and I worked with Bull Moose Jackson, and he had a big band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Norris Turney interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: the first job I had after the truck driver was Bullmoose Jackson... That’s when I joined them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: the first job I had after the truck driver was Bullmoose Jackson... That’s when I joined them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Al Grey
about_quotes.evidence: From Jimmie Lunceford, I went with Lucky Millinder’s orchestra, who had Lil Green and many — Bull Moose Jackson and all of these great artists.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Grey interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: I said, 'You wouldn’t be Bull Moose Jackson would you?' He said, 'Look here! This boy knows me!'
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: I said, “Benny, might have a guest tonight. I saw Bull Moose.” ... And he’s coming up to the stage, walked up on the stage and said, “Alright! Right here!”
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
